meta.title: WISE RC13 Expansion Candidates
meta.note: Candidates to close geographic/cultural/biodiversity gaps; merged into top_200_global_assets.json. Per ADR-010 never gate canonical memory.

candidates.title: Great Zimbabwe
candidates.historical_significance: Largest medieval stone city in sub-Saharan Africa, capital of a gold-trading kingdom.

candidates.title: Rock-Hewn Churches of Lalibela
candidates.historical_significance: Eleven monolithic churches carved from rock, a living pilgrimage site.

candidates.title: Timbuktu
candidates.historical_significance: Medieval centre of Islamic scholarship and trans-Saharan trade.

candidates.title: Pyramids of Meroe
candidates.historical_significance: Steep Nubian royal pyramids of the Kingdom of Kush.

candidates.title: Benin Bronzes
candidates.historical_significance: Masterworks of West African brass casting from the Kingdom of Benin.

candidates.title: Nok Terracotta
candidates.historical_significance: Among the oldest sculptural traditions of sub-Saharan Africa.

candidates.title: Ngorongoro Crater
candidates.historical_significance: Vast volcanic caldera teeming with East African wildlife.

candidates.title: Okavango Delta
candidates.historical_significance: Inland delta and one of Africa's richest wildlife habitats.

candidates.title: Mount Kenya
candidates.historical_significance: Africa's second-highest peak, a glaciated equatorial massif.

candidates.title: Giraffe (Giraffa)
candidates.historical_significance: Tallest living animal, an icon of the African savanna.

candidates.title: Black Rhinoceros (Diceros bicornis)
candidates.historical_significance: Critically endangered browser, a flagship of anti-poaching efforts.

candidates.title: Hippopotamus (Hippopotamus amphibius)
candidates.historical_significance: Massive semi-aquatic mammal of Africa's rivers.

candidates.title: Chimpanzee (Pan troglodytes)
candidates.historical_significance: Our closest living relative alongside the bonobo.

candidates.title: Leopard (Panthera pardus)
candidates.historical_significance: Widest-ranging big cat, elusive and powerful.

candidates.title: Plains Zebra (Equus quagga)
candidates.historical_significance: Striped icon of the great African migrations.

candidates.title: Robben Island
candidates.historical_significance: Prison island and symbol of the struggle against apartheid.

candidates.title: Persepolis
candidates.historical_significance: Ceremonial capital of the Achaemenid Persian Empire.

candidates.title: Ishtar Gate of Babylon
candidates.historical_significance: Glazed-brick gate of ancient Babylon, a Mesopotamian masterpiece.

candidates.title: Standard of Ur
candidates.historical_significance: Sumerian mosaic box depicting war and peace, c. 2600 BCE.

candidates.title: Palmyra
candidates.historical_significance: Caravan city of the Syrian desert blending Greco-Roman and Persian art.

candidates.title: Gobekli Tepe
candidates.historical_significance: World's oldest known monumental sanctuary, predating agriculture.

candidates.title: Ephesus
candidates.historical_significance: Great Greco-Roman city, home to the Library of Celsus.

candidates.title: Dome of the Rock
candidates.historical_significance: Iconic gold-domed Islamic shrine on the Temple Mount.

candidates.title: Naqsh-e Jahan Square
candidates.historical_significance: Safavid royal square in Isfahan ringed by tiled masterpieces.

candidates.title: Shahnameh Illustrated Folios
candidates.historical_significance: Persian miniature paintings from the Book of Kings.

candidates.title: Ziggurat of Ur
candidates.historical_significance: Restored Sumerian temple-tower of the moon god.

candidates.title: Baalbek
candidates.historical_significance: Colossal Roman temple complex in the Beqaa Valley.

candidates.title: Arabian Oryx (Oryx leucoryx)
candidates.historical_significance: Desert antelope brought back from extinction in the wild.

candidates.title: Cedar of Lebanon (Cedrus libani)
candidates.historical_significance: Ancient sacred tree and national emblem of Lebanon.

candidates.title: Krak des Chevaliers
candidates.historical_significance: Best-preserved Crusader castle in the world.

candidates.title: Registan, Samarkand
candidates.historical_significance: Ensemble of tiled madrasas at the heart of the Silk Road.

candidates.title: Itchan Kala (Khiva)
candidates.historical_significance: Walled inner town of the Khivan oasis city.

candidates.title: Po-i-Kalyan, Bukhara
candidates.historical_significance: Minaret and mosque complex of the holy city of Bukhara.

candidates.title: Buddhas of Bamiyan
candidates.historical_significance: Colossal cliff-carved Buddhas of the Silk Road (destroyed 2001).

candidates.title: Lake Issyk-Kul
candidates.historical_significance: Vast endorheic alpine lake ringed by the Tian Shan.

candidates.title: Tian Shan Mountains
candidates.historical_significance: The 'celestial mountains' spanning Central Asia.

candidates.title: Bactrian Camel (Camelus bactrianus)
candidates.historical_significance: Two-humped camel of the Silk Road steppes.

candidates.title: Pamir Highlands
candidates.historical_significance: The 'Roof of the World' high-altitude plateau.

candidates.title: Ajanta and Ellora Caves
candidates.historical_significance: Rock-cut Buddhist, Hindu and Jain cave temples with ancient murals.

candidates.title: Khajuraho Temples
candidates.historical_significance: Medieval temples renowned for intricate sculpture.

candidates.title: Varanasi Ghats
candidates.historical_significance: Sacred riverfront steps on the Ganges, a Hindu pilgrimage heart.

candidates.title: Hampi
candidates.historical_significance: Boulder-strewn ruins of the Vijayanagara Empire capital.

candidates.title: Sigiriya
candidates.historical_significance: Rock fortress crowned by a royal citadel and frescoes.

candidates.title: Sanchi Stupa
candidates.historical_significance: One of the oldest stone Buddhist monuments in India.

candidates.title: Brihadeeswarar Temple
candidates.historical_significance: Chola granite temple, a Dravidian architectural triumph.

candidates.title: Paro Taktsang (Tiger's Nest)
candidates.historical_significance: Cliffside Himalayan monastery, an emblem of Bhutan.

candidates.title: Meenakshi Temple
candidates.historical_significance: Towering, sculpture-covered gopurams of Madurai.

candidates.title: Asian Elephant (Elephas maximus)
candidates.historical_significance: Sacred and endangered elephant of Asian cultures.

candidates.title: Indian Peafowl (Pavo cristatus)
candidates.historical_significance: National bird of India, famed for its iridescent train.

candidates.title: One-horned Rhinoceros (Rhinoceros unicornis)
candidates.historical_significance: Armoured greater rhino of the Indian floodplains.

candidates.title: Himalayan Monal (Lophophorus impejanus)
candidates.historical_significance: Iridescent pheasant of the high Himalaya.

candidates.title: Mount Everest
candidates.historical_significance: Earth's highest peak, the summit of the Himalaya.

candidates.title: Sydney Opera House
candidates.historical_significance: Expressionist sail-roofed icon of modern architecture.

candidates.title: Uluru-Kata Tjuta
candidates.historical_significance: Sacred monolith of the Anangu in the Australian outback.

candidates.title: Twelve Apostles
candidates.historical_significance: Limestone sea stacks along the Great Ocean Road.

candidates.title: Aoraki / Mount Cook
candidates.historical_significance: New Zealand's highest peak amid the Southern Alps.

candidates.title: Milford Sound
candidates.historical_significance: Sheer-walled fiord of Fiordland, a natural wonder.

candidates.title: Kakadu Rock Art
candidates.historical_significance: Aboriginal rock paintings spanning tens of thousands of years.

candidates.title: Maori Meeting House
candidates.historical_significance: Carved communal house (wharenui) at the heart of Maori life.

candidates.title: Red Kangaroo (Osphranter rufus)
candidates.historical_significance: Largest marsupial and emblem of Australia.

candidates.title: Platypus (Ornithorhynchus anatinus)
candidates.historical_significance: Egg-laying monotreme, one of evolution's marvels.

candidates.title: Kiwi (Apteryx)
candidates.historical_significance: Flightless nocturnal bird and national symbol of New Zealand.

candidates.title: Nan Madol
candidates.historical_significance: Megalithic stone city built on a Pacific lagoon.

candidates.title: Teotihuacan
candidates.historical_significance: Vast Mesoamerican city of the Pyramids of the Sun and Moon.

candidates.title: Nazca Lines
candidates.historical_significance: Monumental desert geoglyphs of the Nazca culture.

candidates.title: Mesa Verde Cliff Palace
candidates.historical_significance: Ancestral Puebloan cliff dwellings of the Southwest.

candidates.title: Cahokia Mounds
candidates.historical_significance: Largest pre-Columbian earthwork city north of Mexico.

candidates.title: Tiwanaku
candidates.historical_significance: Andean ceremonial capital near Lake Titicaca.

candidates.title: Moche Portrait Vessels
candidates.historical_significance: Strikingly realistic ceramic portraits of the Moche.

candidates.title: Haida Totem Poles
candidates.historical_significance: Monumental carved cedar poles of the Pacific Northwest.

candidates.title: Inuksuk
candidates.historical_significance: Inuit stone landmarks of the Arctic.

candidates.title: Caral
candidates.historical_significance: Oldest known city in the Americas.

candidates.title: Chaco Canyon
candidates.historical_significance: Great houses and astronomy of the Ancestral Puebloans.

candidates.title: Sami Cultural Heritage
candidates.historical_significance: Living reindeer-herding culture of the European Arctic.

candidates.title: Aboriginal Dreaming Paintings
candidates.historical_significance: Dot and Dreamtime paintings of Aboriginal Australia.

candidates.title: Orca (Orcinus orca)
candidates.historical_significance: Ocean's apex predator, the largest dolphin.

candidates.title: Galapagos Giant Tortoise (Chelonoidis niger)
candidates.historical_significance: Long-lived tortoise central to evolutionary science.

candidates.title: Scarlet Macaw (Ara macao)
candidates.historical_significance: Brilliant neotropical parrot of the rainforest canopy.

candidates.title: Greater Flamingo (Phoenicopterus roseus)
candidates.historical_significance: Pink wading bird of saline lakes and lagoons.

candidates.title: Pangolin (Manis)
candidates.historical_significance: Scaled mammal, the world's most trafficked animal.

candidates.title: Three-toed Sloth (Bradypus)
candidates.historical_significance: Slow-moving icon of the neotropical canopy.

candidates.title: Reindeer (Rangifer tarandus)
candidates.historical_significance: Migratory deer central to Arctic cultures.

candidates.title: Narwhal (Monodon monoceros)
candidates.historical_significance: Tusked 'unicorn of the sea'.

candidates.title: Saltwater Crocodile (Crocodylus porosus)
candidates.historical_significance: Largest living reptile, an estuarine apex predator.

candidates.title: Golden Eagle (Aquila chrysaetos)
candidates.historical_significance: Powerful raptor revered across the Northern Hemisphere.

candidates.title: Manta Ray (Mobula birostris)
candidates.historical_significance: Gliding giant of tropical seas.

candidates.title: Reef-building Coral
candidates.historical_significance: Foundation of reef ecosystems and a climate sentinel.

candidates.title: Babylonian Map of the World
candidates.historical_significance: Earliest known world map (Imago Mundi), c. 600 BCE.

candidates.title: Catalan Atlas (1375)
candidates.historical_significance: Lavish medieval atlas of the known world.

candidates.title: Ortelius Theatrum Orbis Terrarum (1570)
candidates.historical_significance: The first modern atlas.

candidates.title: Piri Reis Map (1513)
candidates.historical_significance: Ottoman world map preserving early American coastlines.

candidates.title: Cantino Planisphere (1502)
candidates.historical_significance: Earliest surviving map of Portuguese discoveries.

candidates.title: Hevelius Selenographia (1647)
candidates.historical_significance: First detailed atlas of the Moon.

candidates.title: Ptolemy's Geographia
candidates.historical_significance: Renaissance reconstruction of the classical world map.

candidates.title: Diamond Sutra (Dunhuang)
candidates.historical_significance: World's earliest dated printed book (868 CE).

candidates.title: Statue of Liberty
candidates.historical_significance: Neoclassical monument and global symbol of freedom.

candidates.title: Saint Basil's Cathedral
candidates.historical_significance: Riot of colourful onion domes on Moscow's Red Square.

candidates.title: Ha Long Bay
candidates.historical_significance: Emerald waters studded with thousands of limestone karsts.

candidates.title: Yosemite
candidates.historical_significance: Granite cliffs, waterfalls and giant sequoias.

candidates.title: Torres del Paine
candidates.historical_significance: Dramatic Patagonian peaks, glaciers and lakes.
